title: stylidium foveolatum
intro: Stylidium foveolatum, also known as the spatter dot, is a small perennial plant that belongs to the family Stylidiaceae and is native to Western Australia. Traditionally, this plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Stylidium foveolatum include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and treat various infections. The herbal preparations of Stylidium foveolatum typically involve making a decoction from the plant's leaves and roots, which can be used as a topical application or ingested as a tea.
